title: ～極まりない
titleEn: Expresses an extreme state, often negative; 'utterly', 'extremely', 'boundlessly'.
structure: な-Adjective（語幹）+ 極まりない, い-Adjective（連用形）+ こと + 極まりない, Noun + の極まりない
structureEn: な-Adjective（語幹）+ 極まりない, い-Adjective（連用形）+ こと + 極まりない, Noun + の極まりない
explanation: Expresses an extreme state, often negative; 'utterly', 'extremely', 'boundlessly'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～極まりない grammar point indicates an extreme or utmost degree, most commonly with a negative nuance (e.g., 失礼極まりない, 危険極まりない). It can sometimes be used for positive contexts, but that usage is less common.

title: ～極まる
titleEn: Also expresses an extreme degree or state, typically negative.
structure: な-Adjective（語幹）+ 極まる, い-Adjective（連用形）+ こと + 極まる, etc.
structureEn: な-Adjective（語幹）+ 極まる, い-Adjective（連用形）+ こと + 極まる, etc.
explanation: Also expresses an extreme degree or state, typically negative. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～極まる form often appears with な-adjectives: e.g., 失礼極まる, 非常識極まる ('extremely rude/unreasonable'). It's largely synonymous with ～極まりない but tends to appear in set phrases. Both forms are quite formal and emphatic.

title: ～足りない
structure: Noun + (が) 足りない, Verb-casual + ほど/だけ + (が) 足りない
structureEn: Noun + (が) 足りない, Verb-casual + ほど/だけ + (が) 足りない
explanation: Expresses insufficiency or lack; 'not enough', 'insufficient'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～足りない pattern simply means that something is not sufficient in quantity, time, detail, ability, etc. It’s the negative form of 足りる ('to be enough').

title: ～足る Noun
titleEn: Indicates that something is worthy or sufficient to be called/used as ~; 'worthy of', 'enough to ~'.
explanation: Indicates that something is worthy or sufficient to be called/used as ~; 'worthy of', 'enough to ~'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The classical pattern 'Noun + に足る + Noun' or 'Verb + に足る + Noun' means 'something is worthy of or sufficient for ~.' For instance, '信頼に足る人物' means 'a person worthy of trust.' It’s different from the everyday verb 足りる ('to be enough').

title: ～限りだ
titleEn: Expresses an extreme degree of emotion or reaction; 'I feel so ~', 'as ~ as can be'.
structure: い-Adjective + 限りだ, な-Adjective + な/である + 限りだ, Noun + の + 限りだ
structureEn: い-Adjective + 限りだ, な-Adjective + な/である + 限りだ, Noun + の + 限りだ
explanation: Expresses an extreme degree of emotion or reaction; 'I feel so ~', 'as ~ as can be'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～限りだ structure is often used with adjectives describing the speaker's emotion, highlighting how strongly the speaker feels. Translations often include 'I’m as ~ as can be,' 'I feel so ~.'
